# fabricate - Massively Parallel Project Fabrication Engine

Transform opus documentation suites into complete project implementations through multi-dimensional parallel agent orchestration.

Usage: `/fabricate [project-path]` - Execute parallel fabrication using opus documentation as comprehensive schematics for systematic project generation.

**Prerequisites:** An opus documentation suite must exist (generated by `/opus`). The command discovers all opus documents and orchestrates massively parallel implementation.

---

## Parallel Fabrication Architecture

```
                         ┌──────────────────────┐
                         │    /fabricate        │
                         │    <project-path>    │
                         └──────────┬───────────┘
                                    │
                    ┌───────────────▼───────────────┐
                    │  Phase 1: Parallel Analysis   │
                    │  ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐     │
                    │  │Doc 1│ │Doc 2│ │Doc N│     │
                    │  └──┬──┘ └──┬──┘ └──┬──┘     │
                    └─────┼───────┼───────┼────────┘
                          └───────┼───────┘
                                  ▼
                    ┌───────────────────────────────┐
                    │  Phase 2: Parallel Strategy   │
                    │  ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ │
                    │  │Lang│ │Frmw│ │Arch│ │Test│ │
                    │  └──┬─┘ └──┬─┘ └──┬─┘ └──┬─┘ │
                    └─────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼───┘
                          └──────┼──────┘──────┘
                                 ▼
                    ┌───────────────────────────────┐
                    │  Phase 3: Parallel Structure  │
                    │  ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ │
                    │  │Dirs│ │Conf│ │VCS │ │Deps│ │
                    │  └──┬─┘ └──┬─┘ └──┬─┘ └──┬─┘ │
                    └─────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼───┘
                          └──────┼──────┘──────┘
                                 ▼
         ┌───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┐
         │                       │                       │
    ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐
    │Module 1 │ │Module 2 │ │Module 3 │ │Module N │ │Module M │
    │Engineer │ │Engineer │ │Engineer │ │Engineer │ │Engineer │
    └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘
         │           │           │           │           │
         └───────────┴───────────┼───────────┴───────────┘
                                 ▼
         ┌───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┐
         │                       │                       │
    ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐
    │  CLI    │ │ Config  │ │ Deploy  │ │  Docs   │ │ Monitor │
    │ Agent   │ │ Agent   │ │ Agent   │ │ Agent   │ │ Agent   │
    └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘
         │           │           │           │           │
         └───────────┴───────────┼───────────┴───────────┘
                                 ▼
         ┌───────────────────────┼───────────────────────┐
         │                       │                       │
    ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐ ┌────▼────┐
    │ Unit    │ │ Integ   │ │Security │ │ Perf    │ │ Spec    │
    │ Tester  │ │ Tester  │ │ Auditor │ │ Tester  │ │Validator│
    └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘
         │           │           │           │           │
         └───────────┴───────────┼───────────┴───────────┘
                                 ▼
                    ┌───────────────────────────────┐
                    │   Master Synthesis Agent      │
                    │   Final Integration & Report  │
                    └───────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Sequential Protocol with Massive Parallelism

### Phase 1: Parallel Documentation Analysis

**Objective:** Simultaneously analyze all opus documents for comprehensive understanding.

**Parallel Execution Protocol:**

```
# Spawn parallel analyzers for each opus document
FOR EACH document IN [README, PURPOSE, INTENT, CONCEPTS, METHODS, CLAUDE, SPECIFICATION]:
    SPAWN Task(
        subagent_type: "general-purpose",
        prompt: document_analysis_prompt(document),
        run_in_background: false  # Parallel but tracked
    )
```

**Agent Distribution:**
| Agent | Document | Extraction Focus |
|-------|----------|------------------|
| Analyzer-1 | README.md | Project overview, quick start, dependencies |
| Analyzer-2 | PURPOSE.md | Mission, objectives, success metrics |
| Analyzer-3 | INTENT.md | User goals, use cases, UX requirements |
| Analyzer-4 | CONCEPTS.md | Terminology, principles, architecture patterns |
| Analyzer-5 | METHODS.md | Implementation approaches, testing strategies |
| Analyzer-6 | CLAUDE.md | AI collaboration patterns, agent instructions |
| Analyzer-7 | SPECIFICATION.md | Technical requirements, API specs, data models |

**Agent Instructions Template:**
```markdown
You are analyzing a single opus document for project fabrication.

**Document:** {document.path}
**Document Type:** {document.type}

**Extract and Structure:**
1. Core requirements and specifications
2. Technical constraints and dependencies
3. Implementation hints and patterns
4. Quality criteria and validation rules
5. Cross-references to other opus documents

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "document": "{document.type}",
  "requirements": [...],
  "constraints": [...],
  "patterns": [...],
  "quality_criteria": [...],
  "cross_references": [...],
  "implementation_hints": {...}
}
```

**Synthesis Checkpoint:**
- Collect all 7 analysis results
- Merge into unified `fabrication_spec` object
- Validate cross-document consistency
- Resolve conflicting specifications

---

### Phase 2: Parallel Strategy Determination

**Objective:** Simultaneously determine all architectural and implementation decisions.

**Parallel Execution Protocol:**

```
# Spawn parallel strategists for each decision domain
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: language_strategy_prompt(fabrication_spec))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: framework_strategy_prompt(fabrication_spec))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: architecture_strategy_prompt(fabrication_spec))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: testing_strategy_prompt(fabrication_spec))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: deployment_strategy_prompt(fabrication_spec))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: security_strategy_prompt(fabrication_spec))
```

**Agent Distribution:**
| Agent | Domain | Decisions |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| Strategist-Lang | Language | Primary language, version, toolchain |
| Strategist-Framework | Frameworks | Web framework, ORM, utility libraries |
| Strategist-Arch | Architecture | Pattern (monolith/micro/serverless), structure |
| Strategist-Test | Testing | Frameworks, coverage targets, CI integration |
| Strategist-Deploy | Deployment | Target environment, containerization, orchestration |
| Strategist-Security | Security | Auth patterns, encryption, vulnerability prevention |

**Agent Instructions Template:**
```markdown
You are a {domain} strategist for project fabrication.

**Fabrication Spec:** {fabrication_spec}

**Your Domain:** {domain}

**Determine:**
1. Optimal choices for your domain based on spec requirements
2. Compatibility with other likely domain decisions
3. Trade-offs and rationale for recommendations
4. Configuration requirements and dependencies

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "domain": "{domain}",
  "primary_choice": "...",
  "alternatives": [...],
  "rationale": "...",
  "dependencies": [...],
  "configuration": {...},
  "compatibility_notes": [...]
}
```

**Synthesis Checkpoint:**
- Merge all strategy outputs into `implementation_plan`
- Validate cross-domain compatibility
- Resolve conflicts (e.g., framework vs language constraints)
- Generate unified technology stack specification

---

### Phase 3: Parallel Structure Generation

**Objective:** Simultaneously create all foundational project elements.

**Parallel Execution Protocol:**

```
# Spawn parallel generators for each structural element
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: directory_structure_prompt(implementation_plan))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: config_files_prompt(implementation_plan))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: vcs_setup_prompt(implementation_plan))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: dependency_manifest_prompt(implementation_plan))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: dev_environment_prompt(implementation_plan))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: documentation_scaffold_prompt(implementation_plan))
```

**Agent Distribution:**
| Agent | Element | Outputs |
|-------|---------|---------|
| Generator-Dirs | Directory Structure | Full hierarchy with README stubs |
| Generator-Config | Configuration | .env templates, config files, settings |
| Generator-VCS | Version Control | .gitignore, hooks, branch strategy |
| Generator-Deps | Dependencies | package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml |
| Generator-DevEnv | Dev Environment | Docker, devcontainer, Makefile |
| Generator-Docs | Documentation | README sync, API doc scaffolds, guides |

**Agent Instructions Template:**
```markdown
You are generating {element} for project fabrication.

**Implementation Plan:** {implementation_plan}
**Project Path:** {project_path}

**Generate:**
1. Complete {element} appropriate for the technology stack
2. Industry best practices and conventions
3. Extensibility hooks for future additions
4. Comments and documentation within files

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "element": "{element}",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "relative/path/to/file",
      "content": "file content here",
      "permissions": "644"
    }
  ],
  "directories": ["path/to/dir1", "path/to/dir2"],
  "post_creation_commands": ["npm install", "..."]
}
```

**File Creation Protocol:**
- Collect all generator outputs
- Deduplicate and merge directory structures
- Create directories first, then files
- Execute post-creation commands in dependency order

---

### Phase 4: Massive Parallel Feature Implementation

**Objective:** Simultaneously implement all modules, features, and components.

**Module Extraction:**
```
# Extract implementable modules from fabrication_spec
modules = extract_modules(fabrication_spec)
# Typically includes: core, api, models, services, utils, handlers, middleware, etc.
```

**Parallel Execution Protocol:**

```
# Spawn engineer agent for EACH module simultaneously
FOR EACH module IN modules:
    SPAWN Task(
        subagent_type: "general-purpose",
        prompt: module_implementation_prompt(module, implementation_plan),
        run_in_background: false
    )
```

**Agent Distribution (Dynamic based on spec):**
| Agent | Module | Implementation Scope |
|-------|--------|---------------------|
| Engineer-Core | core/ | Core business logic, domain models |
| Engineer-API | api/ | API endpoints, request handlers, routing |
| Engineer-Models | models/ | Data models, schemas, validation |
| Engineer-Services | services/ | Business services, external integrations |
| Engineer-Utils | utils/ | Utility functions, helpers, formatters |
| Engineer-Handlers | handlers/ | Event handlers, middleware, hooks |
| Engineer-Auth | auth/ | Authentication, authorization, sessions |
| Engineer-DB | database/ | Database connections, migrations, queries |
| Engineer-N | {module_n}/ | Additional modules as specified |

**Engineer Agent Instructions Template:**
```markdown
You are an engineer implementing the {module.name} module.

**Module Specification:**
{module.spec}

**Implementation Plan:**
{implementation_plan}

**Technology Stack:**
- Language: {stack.language}
- Framework: {stack.framework}
- Testing: {stack.testing}

**Your Task:**
1. Implement all components specified for {module.name}
2. Follow the architecture patterns from the implementation plan
3. Include comprehensive error handling
4. Add inline documentation and type hints
5. Create corresponding test files (unit tests)
6. Ensure integration points with other modules are well-defined

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "module": "{module.name}",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "src/{module.name}/component.{ext}",
      "content": "implementation code",
      "type": "source"
    },
    {
      "path": "tests/{module.name}/test_component.{ext}",
      "content": "test code",
      "type": "test"
    }
  ],
  "exports": ["public_function_1", "ClassName"],
  "dependencies": ["other_module.function"],
  "integration_points": [
    {
      "module": "other_module",
      "interface": "function_signature",
      "purpose": "description"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Batch Synthesis - Integration Coherence:**
```
# Group module outputs into integration batches
batches = partition(module_outputs, batch_size=4)

FOR EACH batch IN batches:
    SPAWN Task(
        subagent_type: "general-purpose",
        prompt: integration_synthesis_prompt(batch)
    )
```

**Synthesis Agent Instructions:**
```markdown
You are an integration synthesizer ensuring module coherence.

**Modules in Batch:** {batch.module_names}
**Module Outputs:** {batch.outputs}

**Verify:**
1. Import/export compatibility between modules
2. Interface contract consistency
3. No circular dependencies
4. Consistent error handling patterns
5. Unified logging and observability approach

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "coherence_score": 0-100,
  "integration_issues": [...],
  "required_adjustments": [...],
  "dependency_graph": {...},
  "interface_contracts": {...}
}
```

---

### Phase 5: Parallel Integration Systems

**Objective:** Simultaneously implement all integration and enhancement systems.

**Parallel Execution Protocol:**

```
# Spawn integration agents for each system
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: cli_implementation_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: config_management_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: deployment_scripts_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: cicd_pipeline_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: documentation_sync_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: monitoring_observability_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: api_documentation_prompt(...))
```

**Agent Distribution:**
| Agent | System | Outputs |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Integrator-CLI | CLI Interface | Command implementations, help system, arg parsing |
| Integrator-Config | Config Management | Environment handling, secrets, feature flags |
| Integrator-Deploy | Deployment | Docker, K8s manifests, deployment scripts |
| Integrator-CICD | CI/CD Pipeline | GitHub Actions, testing workflows, release automation |
| Integrator-Docs | Documentation | API docs, user guides, developer documentation |
| Integrator-Monitor | Observability | Logging, metrics, health checks, alerting |
| Integrator-APIDoc | API Documentation | OpenAPI/Swagger specs, endpoint documentation |

**Agent Instructions Template:**
```markdown
You are implementing the {system} integration for the fabricated project.

**Project Structure:** {project_structure}
**Module Interfaces:** {module_interfaces}
**Implementation Plan:** {implementation_plan}

**Implement:**
1. Complete {system} appropriate for the project
2. Integration with all implemented modules
3. Industry best practices and standards
4. Configuration options and extensibility

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "system": "{system}",
  "files": [...],
  "configuration": {...},
  "integration_hooks": [...],
  "usage_examples": [...]
}
```

---

### Phase 6: Massive Parallel Validation

**Objective:** Simultaneously execute all validation, testing, and quality assurance.

**Parallel Execution Protocol:**

```
# Spawn validation agents for each quality dimension
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: unit_test_execution_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: integration_test_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: security_audit_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: performance_validation_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: spec_compliance_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: documentation_accuracy_prompt(...))
SPAWN Task(subagent_type: "general-purpose", prompt: code_quality_prompt(...))
```

**Agent Distribution:**
| Agent | Validation Dimension | Scope |
|-------|---------------------|-------|
| Validator-Unit | Unit Tests | Execute all unit tests, coverage report |
| Validator-Integration | Integration Tests | Cross-module integration validation |
| Validator-Security | Security Audit | OWASP checks, dependency vulnerabilities |
| Validator-Performance | Performance | Load testing, benchmark validation |
| Validator-Spec | Specification Compliance | Opus spec vs implementation alignment |
| Validator-Docs | Documentation Accuracy | Docs match implementation |
| Validator-Quality | Code Quality | Linting, formatting, complexity metrics |

**Agent Instructions Template:**
```markdown
You are validating {dimension} for the fabricated project.

**Project Path:** {project_path}
**Fabrication Spec:** {fabrication_spec}
**Implementation Summary:** {implementation_summary}

**Validate:**
1. Execute {dimension} validation procedures
2. Generate detailed report with findings
3. Classify issues by severity (critical, warning, info)
4. Provide remediation recommendations

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "dimension": "{dimension}",
  "status": "pass|fail|warning",
  "score": 0-100,
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "critical|warning|info",
      "location": "file:line",
      "issue": "description",
      "remediation": "fix suggestion"
    }
  ],
  "metrics": {...},
  "recommendations": [...]
}
```

---

### Phase 7: Master Synthesis & Deployment Readiness

**Objective:** Aggregate all parallel outputs and produce final integrated project.

**Execution Protocol:**

```
SPAWN Task(
    subagent_type: "general-purpose",
    prompt: master_synthesis_prompt(all_phase_outputs)
)
```

**Master Synthesizer Instructions:**
```markdown
You are the master synthesizer completing project fabrication.

**All Phase Outputs:**
- Phase 1 Analysis: {analysis_outputs}
- Phase 2 Strategy: {strategy_outputs}
- Phase 3 Structure: {structure_outputs}
- Phase 4 Implementation: {implementation_outputs}
- Phase 5 Integration: {integration_outputs}
- Phase 6 Validation: {validation_outputs}

**Your Objectives:**
1. Resolve any remaining integration issues
2. Apply final coherence fixes across all files
3. Ensure all cross-references are valid
4. Generate final project state
5. Create deployment readiness report

**Output Format (JSON):**
{
  "final_files": [...],
  "final_adjustments": [...],
  "project_statistics": {
    "total_files": N,
    "lines_of_code": N,
    "test_coverage": "%",
    "modules_implemented": N,
    "spec_compliance": "%"
  },
  "deployment_readiness": {
    "status": "ready|blocked",
    "blockers": [...],
    "recommendations": [...]
  },
  "fabrication_summary": "..."
}
```

---

## Command Invocation

```bash
# Basic fabrication
/fabricate ./MyProject

# With options
/fabricate ./MyProject --parallelism high --validation comprehensive

# Dry run
/fabricate ./MyProject --dry-run

# Resume from phase
/fabricate ./MyProject --resume-from phase4
```

## Parameters & Options

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `project-path` | path | `.` | Target project directory |
| `--opus-path` | path | auto | Custom opus documentation location |
| `--parallelism` | enum | `high` | Agent parallelism (low\|medium\|high\|maximum) |
| `--validation` | enum | `standard` | Validation depth (minimal\|standard\|comprehensive) |
| `--dry-run` | flag | false | Preview without file creation |
| `--resume-from` | enum | - | Resume from specific phase |
| `--preserve` | glob | - | Files to preserve during fabrication |
| `--verbose` | flag | false | Detailed progress output |

## Parallelism Levels

| Level | Phase 1 | Phase 2 | Phase 3 | Phase 4 | Phase 5 | Phase 6 |
|-------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| low | 3 agents | 2 agents | 2 agents | 3 agents | 3 agents | 3 agents |
| medium | 5 agents | 4 agents | 4 agents | 6 agents | 5 agents | 5 agents |
| high | 7 agents | 6 agents | 6 agents | 10 agents | 7 agents | 7 agents |
| maximum | 7 agents | 6 agents | 6 agents | unlimited | 7 agents | 7 agents |

---

## Quality Assurance

**Per-Phase Validation:**
- ✅ Phase 1: All opus docs parsed, no conflicts
- ✅ Phase 2: Strategy decisions compatible
- ✅ Phase 3: All structure elements created
- ✅ Phase 4: All modules implemented, integration verified
- ✅ Phase 5: All systems operational
- ✅ Phase 6: All validation dimensions pass
- ✅ Phase 7: Master synthesis complete

**Rollback Protocol:**
Each phase creates checkpoint before execution:
1. Phase failure triggers rollback to previous checkpoint
2. Partial progress preserved where safe
3. Detailed failure report generated
4. Resume capability from any phase

---

## Performance Targets

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Phase 1 Completion | <30s | 7 parallel analyzers |
| Phase 2 Completion | <20s | 6 parallel strategists |
| Phase 3 Completion | <15s | 6 parallel generators |
| Phase 4 Completion | <3min | N parallel engineers |
| Phase 5 Completion | <1min | 7 parallel integrators |
| Phase 6 Completion | <1min | 7 parallel validators |
| Total Fabrication | <6min | Standard complexity project |
| Spec Compliance | >95% | Implementation vs opus |
| Test Coverage | >80% | Generated test suites |

---

## Integration with Opus Ecosystem

**Complementary Commands:**
- `/opus` → Generates documentation suite (input to fabricate)
- `/fabricate` → Transforms documentation to implementation
- `/reopus` → Enhances existing documentation suite
- `/refabricate` → Updates implementation from evolved documentation

**Workflow:**
```
/opus "description" → docs → /fabricate → project
                              ↑
/reopus "enhancement" → enhanced docs → /refabricate → updated project
```

---

## Error Handling

| Error | Phase | Resolution |
|-------|-------|------------|
| `OPUS_NOT_FOUND` | 1 | Run `/opus` first |
| `PARSE_FAILURE` | 1 | Check opus document syntax |
| `STRATEGY_CONFLICT` | 2 | Manual resolution or override |
| `STRUCTURE_COLLISION` | 3 | Use `--preserve` for existing files |
| `MODULE_FAILURE` | 4 | Retry specific module agent |
| `INTEGRATION_FAILURE` | 5 | Check module interfaces |
| `VALIDATION_FAILURE` | 6 | Review findings, fix issues |
| `SYNTHESIS_FAILURE` | 7 | Manual integration required |

---

## Implementation Checklist

When executing this command:

- [ ] Discover and validate opus documentation suite
- [ ] Spawn Phase 1 parallel analyzers (7 agents)
- [ ] Synthesize analysis into fabrication_spec
- [ ] Spawn Phase 2 parallel strategists (6 agents)
- [ ] Synthesize strategy into implementation_plan
- [ ] Spawn Phase 3 parallel generators (6 agents)
- [ ] Create project structure and files
- [ ] Extract modules from fabrication_spec
- [ ] Spawn Phase 4 parallel engineers (N agents)
- [ ] Execute integration synthesis batches
- [ ] Spawn Phase 5 parallel integrators (7 agents)
- [ ] Spawn Phase 6 parallel validators (7 agents)
- [ ] Execute master synthesis
- [ ] Generate deployment readiness report
- [ ] Report completion status

---

The fabricate command delivers massively parallel project generation through multi-dimensional agent orchestration, transforming opus documentation suites into complete, production-ready implementations with unprecedented speed and coherence.
